questions.text:
The classical Mendel’s work on his ‘laws of inheritance’ was performed on the organism

(A) Drosophila
(B) Snapdragon
(C) Garden pea
(D) Sweet pea
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Garden pea**

Explanation:
Gregor Johann Mendel performed his genetic experiments on the **garden pea plant (Pisum sativum)** because it has clear contrasting traits and easy controlled pollination.

Option Analysis:
• Drosophila → Fruit fly used later by Thomas Hunt Morgan for chromosome theory.
• Snapdragon → Used in incomplete dominance experiments.
• Garden pea → Correct; Mendel discovered **Law of Segregation and Law of Independent Assortment** here.
• Sweet pea → Used in genetic linkage studies.

Exam Insight:
Mendel studied **7 contrasting characters** such as seed color, seed shape and flower color.

questions.text:
The sugar present in DNA is

(A) Glucose
(B) Fructose
(C) Deoxyribose
(D) Ribose
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Deoxyribose**

Explanation:
DNA stands for **Deoxyribonucleic Acid**, which contains the sugar **deoxyribose**.

Option Analysis:
• Glucose → Simple carbohydrate used in metabolism.
• Fructose → Fruit sugar.
• Deoxyribose → Correct; sugar present in DNA backbone.
• Ribose → Sugar present in RNA.

Exam Tip:
DNA = Deoxyribose sugar
RNA = Ribose sugar.

questions.text:
Hereditary symptoms are moved in the descendant of creatures by

(A) Ribosome
(B) Chromosome
(C) Plasma
(D) Lysosome
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Chromosome**

Explanation:
Chromosomes contain **genes**, which transmit hereditary information from parents to offspring.

Option Analysis:
• Ribosome → Site of protein synthesis.
• Chromosome → Correct; carriers of genetic information.
• Plasma → Cell fluid without genetic control role.
• Lysosome → Organelle for digestion.

Exam Insight:
Humans have **23 pairs of chromosomes (46 total)**.

questions.text:
Which of the following is responsible for controlling hereditary characters of the living cells?

(A) Enzyme
(B) Hormone
(C) RNA
(D) None of the above
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: None of the above**

Explanation:
Hereditary characters are controlled mainly by **genes located on DNA**, not enzymes, hormones or RNA.

questions.text:
The unit of heredity in our body is called

(A) Chromosome
(B) DNA
(C) Gene
(D) Nucleus
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Gene**

Explanation:
A **gene** is the smallest functional unit of heredity responsible for expression of traits.

questions.text:
Gene is

(A) A segment of DNA
(B) A segment of DNA and histone
(C) A segment of DNA, RNA and histone
(D) All of the above
options: A segment of DNA and histone
A segment of DNA, RNA and histone
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: A segment of DNA**

Explanation:
Genes are **specific sequences of DNA** coding for proteins.

questions.text:
Who delivered the principle of ‘jumping gene’?

(A) Gregor Johann Mendel
(B) Tomas Hunt Morgan
(C) Barbara Mc Clintock
(D) Watson and Crick
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Barbara McClintock**

Explanation:
She discovered **transposons (jumping genes)** in maize and received the Nobel Prize.

questions.text:
Which compound present in D.N.A. does not form amino acid?

(A) Adenine
(B) Tyrosine
(C) Guanine
(D) Cytosine
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Tyrosine**

Explanation:
Tyrosine is an **amino acid**, whereas adenine, guanine and cytosine are **nitrogen bases of DNA**.

questions.text:
Which of the following professional(s) are more likely to run the risk of a permanent change in their cell’s DNA?
1. Researchers using carbon 14 isotope
2. X-ray technician
3. Coal miner
4. Dyer and painter

Code
(A) Only 2
(B) 1, 2 and 3
(C) 1, 2 and 4
(D) 1, 3 and 4
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Only 2**

Explanation:
X-ray technicians are exposed to **ionizing radiation**, which can damage DNA and cause mutations.

questions.text:
Which of the following is a source of biofertilizer?

(A) Yeast
(B) Chlorella
(C) Azolla
(D) Mold
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Azolla**

Explanation:
Azolla is an aquatic fern containing **Anabaena cyanobacteria** which fixes atmospheric nitrogen.

questions.text:
Which of the following is a biofertilizer?

(A) Urea
(B) 2,4-D
(C) Streptocycline
(D) Azolla
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Azolla**

Explanation:
Azolla enriches soil with nitrogen and improves soil fertility.

questions.text:
The biofertilizer used as a supplementary food for the cattle, especially for milking bovinces is

(A) Azospirillum
(B) Azolla
(C) Azotobacter
(D) Rhizobium
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Azolla**

Explanation:
Azolla contains **20–25% protein** and is used as animal feed.

questions.text:
Which of these is not correctly matched?

(A) Blue-green algae - Biofertilizer
(B) Chrysopa - Maho-control
(C) Bio 902 - Variety of mustard
(D) Azotobacter - Bioinsecticide
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Azotobacter - Bioinsecticide**

Explanation:
Azotobacter is a **biofertilizer**, not a bioinsecticide.

questions.text:
Which bacteria is responsible for nitrogen fixation in soyabean?

(A) Rhizobium leguminosarum
(B) Rhizobium japonicum
(C) Rhizobium fajioleai
(D) Rhizobium traitolai
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Rhizobium japonicum**

Explanation:
This bacterium forms **root nodules in soybean plants and fixes atmospheric nitrogen**.

questions.text:
2, 4-D is

(A) An insecticide
(B) An explosive
(C) A fungicide
(D) A herbicide
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Herbicide**

Explanation:
2,4-D is widely used to kill **broadleaf weeds**.

questions.text:
Blue-green algae are chiefly used as biofertilizer in the crop of

(A) Wheat
(B) Gram
(C) Paddy
(D) Mustard
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Paddy**

Explanation:
Cyanobacteria such as **Anabaena and Nostoc** fix nitrogen in rice fields.

questions.text:
Blue-green algae is used to supply nitrogen to

(A) Bajra
(B) Maize
(C) Paddy
(D) Wheat
questions.solution: **Correct Answer: Paddy**

questions.text:
Which one of the following is not correctly matched?
Nitrogen fixing Crop agent

(A) Blue-green algae - Paddy
(B) Rhizobium - Pea Leguminisarum
(C) Azotobacter - Wheat
(D) Azolla - Maize
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Azolla - Maize**

Explanation:
Azolla is mainly used in **paddy fields**, not maize.

questions.text:
Azolla gives a good biofertilizer when mixed with

(A) Blue-green algae
(B) Bone meal
(C) Cow dung
(D) Urea
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Blue-green algae**

Explanation:
The combination enhances nitrogen fixation.

questions.text:
Azolla is often used as biofertilizer because it is associated with

(A) Blue-green algae
(B) Red algae
(C) Fungus
(D) Moss
questions.solution:
**Correct Answer: Blue-green algae**

Explanation:
Azolla contains the nitrogen-fixing cyanobacterium **Anabaena azollae**.
